230.title: [UX][install] Desktop launcher (one-file binary) — double-click starts engine + opens dashboard

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

230.body:
## Goal
A non-technical user double-clicks **one file** and gets the working dashboard — no Python, no terminal.

## User story
*As a QA engineer with no dev tools, I download `Cherenkov` for my OS, double-click it, and the dashboard
opens in my browser already connected to a running engine.*

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Package the engine + prebuilt FE into a single executable with **PyInstaller** (`--onefile`), one per OS
  (`cherenkov-windows.exe`, `cherenkov-macos`, `cherenkov-linux`).
- On launch it must: (1) start the FastAPI engine on a free port (default 8000, auto-increment if busy),
  (2) open the user's default browser to the dashboard, (3) show a small console/tray status line, (4) shut
  the engine down cleanly on exit.
- Must run with **no Ollama present** by falling back to demo mode (see the demo-mode issue) so first-run never
  dead-ends.
- Bundle the prebuilt FE `dist/` inside the binary (no `npm`); `cherenkov review` already serves `--web`.

## Files / approach
- New `packaging/launcher.py` (entry: start uvicorn in a thread, `webbrowser.open`, wait, teardown).
- New `packaging/cherenkov.spec` (PyInstaller spec; bundle `cherenkov/web/ui/dist` as data).
- New `packaging/build.sh` (build per-OS); document in `docs/INSTALL.md`.
- CI job (matrix win/mac/linux) that builds and uploads artifacts to a GitHub Release.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] On a clean machine with **no Python and no Ollama**, double-click → dashboard opens, health = online (demo).
- [ ] Closing the window stops the engine (no orphan process / port leak).
- [ ] Artifact size documented; build reproducible via `packaging/build.sh`.

## Verify (raw evidence)
- Build the binary, run on a fresh user profile, screenshot the dashboard + `GET /api/v1/health` 200.

## Out of scope
Native window chrome / installers (separate Tauri issue). Refs: ROADMAP_NEXT Phase 1; closed #178/#204.

231.title: [UX][install] Tauri desktop app + native installers (.msi/.dmg/.AppImage) [target]

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

231.body:
## Goal
Ship a **native desktop app** (own window, app icon, OS installer) wrapping the engine + dashboard — the
polished end-state of the one-file launcher.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Tauri shell that loads the local dashboard URL and runs the engine as a **sidecar** (the PyInstaller binary
  from the launcher issue, or a bundled Python).
- Produce signed installers: `.msi`/`.exe` (Windows), `.dmg` (macOS), `.AppImage`/`.deb` (Linux).
- First-launch permission + firewall prompts handled gracefully; app lifecycle starts/stops the sidecar.
- Auto-update channel (Tauri updater) pointing at GitHub Releases.

## Files / approach
- New `desktop/` (Tauri project): `src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` (sidecar = launcher binary), minimal Rust main
  that spawns sidecar + opens the window, `beforeExit` kills sidecar.
- CI: build + (optionally) sign installers on tagged releases.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Installer runs on each OS; app opens a native window showing the dashboard, engine auto-starts.
- [ ] Quitting the app stops the sidecar; relaunch works.
- [ ] Update check wired to Releases (can be stubbed initially).

## Dependencies
Builds on the one-file launcher issue. `needs-human` for code-signing certs. Refs: ROADMAP_NEXT Phase 1.

232.title: [UX][onboarding] First-run wizard in the UI (doctor-in-UI, engine/model detection, demo-vs-real, sample spec)

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

232.body:
## Goal
A guided **first-run wizard** that gets a brand-new user from launch to their first finding in <10 min,
entirely in the UI — replacing the CLI `cherenkov doctor`/`init`.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
Wizard appears on first launch (gate on `localStorage['cherenkov.onboarded']`) with steps:
1. **Welcome** — one-line what-it-does + Start.
2. **Engine check (doctor-in-UI)** — call a new `GET /api/v1/doctor` returning checks
   (engine reachable, Ollama present + models, target reachable, write perms) each with `ok|warn|fail` +
   a human fix string. Render a checklist; block "Next" only on hard fails, offer **Demo mode** instead.
3. **Mode** — *Try a demo run* (no Ollama, cached) **or** *Use my own spec*.
4. **Pick a spec** — bundled `petstore-v2` / `checkout-gateway` presets, or upload, or URL.
5. **Finish** — deep-links into Setup with the choice pre-filled; sets onboarded flag.

## Files / approach
- New backend route `GET /api/v1/doctor` in `cherenkov/web/api.py` (reuse existing doctor/preflight logic if present).
- New FE `components/OnboardingWizard.tsx` + step components; render from `App.tsx` when not onboarded.
- Reuse `useHealth()` for the live engine check; reuse Toast for feedback.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fresh profile → wizard appears; completing it lands on Setup with a runnable choice.
- [ ] Doctor checklist reflects real engine state; a hard fail offers Demo mode (never a dead end).
- [ ] "Skip / I know what I'm doing" available; flag persists so it doesn't reappear.

## Verify
Screenshot each step on a fresh profile; show `GET /api/v1/doctor` JSON. Refs: ROADMAP_NEXT step 1; closed #179.

233.title: [UX][runtime] In-app engine + model manager (status, start/stop/restart, Ollama detect + pull progress, demo toggle)

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

233.body:
## Goal
Let the user manage the engine and LLM **from the UI** — never the terminal.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- **Status:** the existing NODE STATE pill becomes a real engine indicator (online/offline/busy) driven by `useHealth()`.
- **Models:** a panel (in Settings or a dropdown) listing required models and whether they're present; a **Download**
  button that pulls via a new `POST /api/v1/models/pull` streaming progress (SSE/WS), with a progress bar.
  Surface that config references `deepseek-r1:8b` (deep) + `qwen2.5-vl:7b` (vision) which are **not pulled**.
- **Demo toggle:** a visible switch to run in no-Ollama demo mode.
- **Restart:** a "Restart engine" affordance (best-effort; for desktop build it signals the launcher).

## Files / approach
- Backend: `GET /api/v1/models` (present/missing per tier), `POST /api/v1/models/pull` (stream).
- FE: `components/EngineManager.tsx`; wire into Settings + TopBar pill.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Missing models are shown; Download streams progress to completion; status flips to ready.
- [ ] Demo toggle switches mode without a restart for cached flows.
- [ ] Offline engine shows the overlay (#221) and a Retry that recovers.

## Verify
Record a model pull with the progress bar; screenshot the model list before/after. Refs: closed #181/#202/#203.

234.title: [UX][workflow] Full Setup→Run→Pipeline→Review loop driven entirely from the UI (no CLI) + live WS progress

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

234.body:
## Goal
The core journey must complete **without ever touching a terminal**, with real-time progress.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- **Setup:** drag/drop or pick a spec → configure target URL + optional auth → **Run**. Calls `POST /ingest`
  then `POST /run`. Show validation errors from `/ingest` (400) inline (don't swallow).
- **Pipeline:** subscribe to the live event stream (`WEBSOCKET /ws/live` if present, else SSE) and render
  real stage + percent + per-test status — **replace the simulated `MOCK_PILOT_STEPS` animation** with real events.
  If the WS/run fails, show an error state (not a fake success).
- **Auto-advance:** on completion, route to Review Queue populated from `GET /review/queue`.

## Files
- FE: `SetupScreen.tsx`, `PipelineScreen.tsx`, `App.tsx` run handler, `lib/api.ts` (add WS/SSE client).
- Backend: confirm/forward live events on `/ws/live`; emit stage events from the orchestrator run.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] From a fresh dashboard, a user ingests a spec and runs generation with **zero CLI**.
- [ ] Pipeline shows **real** progress events; failures surface as an error state.
- [ ] Completion lands on a populated Review Queue.

## Verify
Screen-record the full loop; show backend logs for `/ingest`, `/run`, and WS frames. Refs: #223, ROADMAP_NEXT §2.

235.title: [UX][workflow] Review Queue: complete HITL in UI + rejection-reason capture + "Why flagged?" AI explanation

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

235.body:
## Goal
Make the **Findings/Review queue** the one screen that matters — approve/reject/edit with reasons and an
AI explanation, all in the UI, with honest feedback.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Each card: endpoint+method, failing gate, **confidence + plain-language reason**, the generated test, and big
  **Approve / Reject / Classify** buttons + a **"Why was this flagged?"** button (Tier-3 AI explanation, already
  built in `openclaw`/`copilot/triage` — surface via a new `POST /api/v1/review/explain` if not exposed).
- **Reject** opens a reason picker ("intended change" / "too noisy" / "wrong assertion" / free text); persist via
  `POST /review/reject` and store the reason (seed of the learning loop, #182).
- **Honest states:** loading spinners on each action; success/error **toasts** — remove the silent
  `.catch(console.warn)` in `ReviewScreen.tsx` and reconcile optimistic state with the server result.
- Empty state ("no findings — run validate") + error state (queue fetch failed).

## Files
- FE: `ReviewScreen.tsx`; reuse `useToast()`. Backend: ensure `review/explain` + reason persistence.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Approve/Reject/Edit reflect **server** result; failures show an error toast (no false success).
- [ ] Reject captures a reason; it is persisted and retrievable.
- [ ] "Why flagged?" returns a plain-language explanation in a drawer/popover.

## Verify
Record approve + reject-with-reason; show backend state advance (`cherenkov hitl list` or re-fetch). Refs: #222, #182.

237.title: [UX][workflow] Eject suite from the UI — in-browser folder picker / zip download, no terminal

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

237.body:
## Goal
Let users **keep their tests** (anti-lock-in) entirely from the UI.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Eject screen: pick output (use the File System Access API directory picker where available, else stream a
  **.zip download** of the standalone suite). Calls `POST /eject`.
- Show what was emitted (spec files, generated types, `client.ts`, `playwright.config.ts`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`)
  and a "runs natively with `npx playwright test`" note.
- Loading + success/error states; never a silent failure.

## Files
- FE: `EjectScreen.tsx`. Backend: add a `GET /api/v1/eject/download` returning a zip stream (or reuse `/eject` + zip).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] User triggers eject and receives a runnable suite as a download or chosen folder — no CLI.
- [ ] Ejected suite runs standalone (`npx playwright test`) with no CHERENKOV dependency.
- [ ] Errors (locked path, backend down) surface clearly.

## Verify
Download the zip, run it outside CHERENKOV, show the pass output. Refs: regression suite TC-UI-060.

238.title: [UX][workflow] Settings: real persistence + editable engine config (target, model tier, egress) via UI

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

238.body:
## Goal
Make Settings real and useful — configure the engine without editing `cherenkov.toml` by hand.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Replace localStorage-only persistence with a backend `GET/PUT /api/v1/settings` that reads/writes the effective
  config (target URL, model tier mappings, egress policy `none|internal|any`, auth header).
- Validate on save; show success/error toast; reflect changes in subsequent runs.
- Keep a clear read-only view of the active `cherenkov.toml` source for transparency.

## Files
- Backend: `GET/PUT /api/v1/settings` over `cherenkov/core/config.py`. FE: `SettingsScreen.tsx`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Settings persist server-side and survive cache clear / different browser.
- [ ] Changing target/egress/model is honored by the next run.
- [ ] Invalid values are rejected with a clear message.

## Verify
Change target, run, show the run used it. Refs: regression suite UX-C2/UX-H1.

239.title: [UX][honesty] Replace mock screens with live data or explicit MOCK badges (Overview/TruthMap/Signals/Governance/Memory/Explore/Author)

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

239.body:
## Goal
Stop the dashboard from silently showing fake data. Every screen is either live or **clearly labelled MOCK**.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Current reality
10/17 surfaces render `mockData.ts` only: Overview, Truth Map, Signals, Governance, Memory, Explore, Author.
There are no `/governance`, `/memory`, `/signals` endpoints.

## Requirements
For each mock screen, **either**:
- (a) wire to a real endpoint (create it where the data genuinely exists — e.g. Overview readiness from real
  divergence counts; Memory from the reflector store), **or**
- (b) if no real source yet, render a small persistent **"MOCK DATA"** badge + tooltip so demos never mislead QA reviewers.
Track per-screen status in a checklist in this issue.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No screen presents fabricated data without a visible MOCK badge.
- [ ] Overview readiness + Top Divergences are computed from **real** `/divergences`.
- [ ] A per-screen live-vs-mock matrix is kept current (link `docs/qa/BUSINESS_REGRESSION_SUITE.md`).

## Verify
Screenshot each screen showing either live data or the MOCK badge. Refs: #224, regression suite §1.

240.title: [UX][polish] Honest empty/loading/error states + 60-sec guided tour across all tabs

labels.name: agent-ready
labels.description: Self-contained; an agent can start now

milestone.title: UX: One-Click + UI-Only
milestone.description: Make CHERENKOV installable by a non-technical QA user in one click and operable entirely through the dashboard tabs. No terminal, no npm, no Ollama commands. Pairs with docs/ROADMAP_NEXT.md Phase 1 (friction kill).

240.body:
## Goal
Every tab handles the not-happy path gracefully and a first-timer gets a 60-second guided tour.

> **Context for agents.** Real backend endpoints today: `GET /health`, `POST /ingest`, `POST /run`,
> `GET /tests`, `GET /review/queue`, `POST /review/{approve,reject,edit,classify}`, `POST /validate`,
> `POST /eject`, `GET /divergences`, `POST /divergences/act` (all under `/api/v1`). Canonical FE =
> `cherenkov/web/ui` (React 19 + Vite 6 + TS + Tailwind). Backend = `cherenkov/web/api.py`, launched by
> `cherenkov.py review`. Anti-drift rule (HANDOVER.md): finish with **raw evidence** (terminal + screenshots),
> never a summary. Verify FE changes in the running dashboard.

## Requirements
- Standard **empty** ("nothing yet — do X"), **loading** (skeletons, not blank/white), and **error** (what failed + retry)
  states for: Review, Divergences, Pipeline, Eject, Setup. (Backend-offline overlay already shipped, #221.)
- A dismissible **guided tour** (e.g. driver.js-style coachmarks) walking Setup → Run → Review → Eject; gated on a flag.
- Slow-connection: show skeletons instead of a frozen white screen (ux_audit finding #4).

## Files
- FE: shared `EmptyState`/`Skeleton` (exist in `components/ui`) applied per screen; new `components/GuidedTour.tsx`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No tab shows a blank/white screen on load or error; each has empty/loading/error.
- [ ] First-run tour runs once, is skippable, and can be replayed from Help (?).

## Verify
Throttle to Slow 3G and reload — show skeletons; record the tour. Refs: closed #180, ux_audit.
